run_started: The dungeon exhales. Your boots find stone, and the dark leans in.
A run begins. The deep is patient and the deep is hungry.
Torchlight catches dust older than your name. Step lightly.
Somewhere below, a door waits to be opened by you specifically.
The threshold accepts you. It does not promise to give you back.
First breath. The walls seem to be counting.
Welcome, traveler. The map is a rumor; the monsters are not.
Your shadow goes ahead of you. It has been here before.
Begin. The deep keeps its own ledger of every footstep.
run_ended: The deep closes its book on you.
The tally is final. The dungeon turns the page.
A run ends. The corridors forget your face quickly.
Silence claims the chamber. Somewhere, a door swings shut.
The torch gutters out. So does the story.
The deep keeps your name now, whether you wanted it to or not.
End of song. The walls were always listening.
Your last footprint joins the others in the dust.
Done. The dungeon will sleep, briefly, before the next traveler.
entered_room: The room receives you. It has had practice at this.
A new chamber. The air tastes faintly of iron and old paper.
You step into a wider dark. Something exhales somewhere.
The walls open up; the silence does not.
A room. Stones set by hands long abandoned by their owners.
Cold floor under your boots. The ceiling is uninterested.
You enter, and the room makes small adjustments.
Another chamber, another patient audience of stones.
The doorway gives you to the room. The room considers you.
killed_monster: The thing falls. The dungeon barely notes it.
One fewer mouth in the dark. The deep adjusts.
Your weapon ends an argument the creature did not know it was having.
Stillness. Then the small sound of something settling forever.
The monster's last thought, if it had one, is now yours by default.
The body folds. The corridor remembers its quiet.
Killed. The dungeon files this under 'expected'.
It dies the way it lived: in the dark, abruptly.
A red ledger entry. The deep does not balance the books in your favor.
low_hp: Your blood is louder than your footsteps now.
The edges of your vision agree to disagree.
You are running low on the kind of luck that comes in pints.
The dungeon notices you breathing harder. It approves.
One more wrong step and the corridor will keep you.
Your hands are not as steady as they were a room ago.
Low. The deep enjoys this part the most.
A wound speaks for you. It says: turn back, perhaps.
The torch flickers in time with your pulse, and your pulse is unreliable.
found_item: An object, abandoned by someone who is no longer using it.
The dungeon offers a gift. The dungeon never offers nothing.
Your fingers close on something cold and approximately useful.
Found: a small kindness from a previous victim.
The item waits as if it expected you. Perhaps it did.
You lift it. The weight is reassuring; the provenance is not.
An item joins your pack. Your pack is heavier with stories now.
The deep is generous today. The deep is rarely generous twice.
A find. Examine it later, when there is light enough for hope.
descended: You go down. The deep makes room for you.
Another floor. The air is older here.
The stairs end. A new dark begins.
Down. The previous floor closes behind you, perhaps for good.
You descend. The dungeon's heart is somewhere below, still beating.
Lower. The walls are colder and a great deal more attentive.
Another step deeper into the ledger of the deep.
Below. The torch finds new dust to disturb.
You go down, and the deep shifts to receive you.
first_sight: You name the shape in the dark: {name}. It does not answer.
The deep introduces you to {name}, briefly, with poor manners.
Call it {name}, then. The dungeon will not argue the point.
{name}, the corridor offers, and the corridor is rarely wrong.
A new face for the ledger: {name}. Try not to share its fate.
You decide on {name}. Whatever it is, it has decided on you.
{name} — the word arrives first, the danger arrives second.
The thing has a shape now, and the shape has a name: {name}.
You whisper {name} under your breath. The deep is taking notes.
room_entered: The chamber is older than the torch that lights it.
Damp stone, cold air, and something that might once have been laughter.
Dust drapes the corners like a tired curtain.
The room remembers other footsteps; yours are merely the latest.
A faint draft moves the dust but not quite enough to be honest about it.
The walls here have learned to keep secrets.
Your torch finds more shadow than stone in this place.
The ceiling vaults higher than seems strictly necessary.
Something rustles beyond the light. The room declines to explain.
epitaph: Here ends a small fire in a very large dark.
The deep keeps you, as it keeps the others, without complaint.
Your last breath is filed beside the rest. The ledger is content.
What you were is now what the dungeon remembers.
The torch is out. The story remains, in stone, in dust, in nothing.
Walk gently into the long corridor; you have walked worse.
The dark closes its hand. It is a careful hand. It has done this before.
Some doors open only one way. You have learned which.
Sleep now, traveler. The deep keeps watch in your stead.
The candle gutters; the page is turned; the chronicle is closed.
room_entered.crypt: The crypt receives you. The dust here is the dust of saints, mostly.
Stone biers line the walls; their tenants do not stir, this time.
The chamber smells of cold incense and older arithmetic.
Iron-bound shelves hold what the saints could not keep with them.
A reliquary cracks beneath your heel and forgives nothing.
The crypt is patient. The crypt has been patient for centuries.
first_sight.crypt: {name}, the crypt offers, in the polite voice of bones.
The crypt has a name for it, and the name is {name}.
Call it {name}. The saints have called it worse.
The dust whispers: {name}. The dust is rarely wrong about the dead.
{name}, says a hollow tongue under the floor. The crypt agrees.
You name it {name}, and a candle somewhere disagrees with the wind.
room_entered.flooded_sewer: Water laps the walls of the chamber; the ceiling drips counterpoint.
The sewer-room reeks of iron, salt, and something older than both.
Rusted grates speak in the language of corroded oaths.
Black water laps your boots. It has been waiting, perhaps, for company.
The room is flooded ankle-deep; the silence is flooded somewhat deeper.
Fungus blooms where the water meets the wall. It is not friendly fungus.
first_sight.flooded_sewer: The sewer names it for you in a voice of slow water: {name}.
{name}, gargles the dark below the grate.
The flood has decided. It calls the thing {name}.
Call it {name}. The eels of the underworks have already agreed.
{name}, says the rust on the pipes. The rust is correct.
A bubble breaks the surface and offers, almost coherently, {name}.
room_entered.mushroom_forest: Pale fronds bend toward your torchlight as if to listen.
The mushroom-forest hush is thick; spores ride your breath out again.
Bioluminescent caps make a lazy constellation of the ceiling.
Soft as a held breath, the room sighs around you.
The fungal floor yields underfoot; the air is sweet and untrustworthy.
Spore-light pools in the corners; the room feels half-asleep.
first_sight.mushroom_forest: The spores hum its name to you: {name}.
{name}, the mycelium offers, in a voice of root and rumor.
Call it {name}. The forest already has, in the way forests do.
Your torchlight finds {name} between the caps. It does not flinch.
{name}, says a chorus of small wet mouths. They are agreeing politely.
The mushroom-forest names it {name}. The mushroom-forest names everything.
room_entered.bone_library: Shelves of vellum and rib-bone rise like a hush given architecture.
Every spine on every shelf is, in the literal sense, a spine.
Ink-stained skulls watch from the high shelves with bookish patience.
The library is silent in the way only old libraries can be silent.
Candles gutter on rib-cage sconces; the catalogue is alphabetized by despair.
Pages whisper among themselves. The librarian, you suspect, is listening.
first_sight.bone_library: The card catalogue offers a name: {name}, see also: regret.
{name}, says a marginal note in a hand older than the book it haunts.
Call it {name}. The library has already filed it under 'patient'.
A loose page lands at your feet. The page reads: {name}.
{name}, the librarian-bones rule, with the dignity of unpaid work.
The shelves agree on {name}, after some discussion among themselves.
room_entered.whisperhall: The Whisperhall opens for you. Or rather, it stops being closed.
Soft voices argue politely in the corners; none are yours.
The walls of the Whisperhall lean in to listen to your footsteps.
A hush like cloth on cloth; the room is older than the language it whispers in.
Names drift along the high vault; some of them, briefly, are yours.
The Whisperhall recognizes you. You wish, faintly, that it would not.
first_sight.whisperhall: The hall offers {name} the way a mouth offers a secret.
{name}, says the room itself, with the intimacy of a held breath.
Call it {name}. The Whisperhall already has, in private.
{name}: the word arrives in three voices and leaves in one.
A hundred whispers conspire on a single name and produce {name}.
{name}, the hall confides. The hall is in the habit of confiding.
